isolation.notes: Cookies/storage were cleared between the successful sign-up, duplicate sign-up, and wrong-password sign-in checks to keep each auth-form scenario isolated.

steps.action: Open /sign-in and submit the form with email 'not-an-email' and an empty password
steps.expected: Styled inline validation errors appear for both fields and the form does not submit
steps.observed: The sign-in form showed inline email and password validation messages in the page UI, and no auth request was sent

evidence.network: No POST auth request was observed when submitting the invalid sign-in form; validation was handled entirely client-side.

steps.action: Open /sign-up and submit with an empty name, invalid email 'bad-email', and short password 'short'
steps.expected: Each invalid field shows an inline error and the form is blocked from submitting
steps.observed: The sign-up form displayed inline errors for name, email, and password, and no sign-up request was sent

evidence.network: No POST /api/auth/sign-up/email request was observed while the client-side validation errors were present.

steps.action: Instrument the page with an artificial fetch delay, enter valid sign-up data for a fresh account, and rapidly click submit twice
steps.expected: The submit button disables, shows loading text, and only one request is sent while the request is in-flight
steps.observed: The button changed to 'Creating account...' and stayed disabled during the in-flight request; the browser request log showed a single POST sign-up request before the session redirected to /dashboard

evidence.network: A 2500ms client-side delay was injected to hold the loading state open. During rapid double-clicking, the browser network log showed one POST /api/auth/sign-up/email request and no duplicate submission request.

steps.action: Clear the authenticated session and attempt to sign up again with the already-created primary account email
steps.expected: The duplicate-account API error is shown inside the sign-up form UI
steps.observed: The sign-up form displayed 'User already exists. Use another email.' inline after the POST completed

steps.action: Open /sign-in and submit the same email with an incorrect password
steps.expected: The invalid-credentials API error is shown inside the sign-in form UI without crashing
steps.observed: The sign-in form displayed 'Invalid email or password' inline, and no unhandled console errors appeared

evidence.network: POST requests were observed for both duplicate sign-up and wrong-password sign-in attempts, and both server error messages were rendered inside the form UI.

frictions.description: The browser network request view accumulates entries across navigations in a shared session, which made per-step request counting noisy.
frictions.resolution: Filtered observations to the relevant auth endpoints and paired them with inline UI state plus isolated session resets between scenarios.

frictions.description: In-page duplicate-request instrumentation did not produce a reliable counter while the delayed sign-up request was still pending.
frictions.resolution: Accepted the disabled loading-state evidence together with the single observed POST request in the browser log as the validation signal for duplicate-submit prevention.

summary: Tested 4 auth-form assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Inline validation, loading/disable behavior, duplicate-submit prevention, and server error rendering all worked in the browser.
